change_description: Added explicit instruction to use exactly ONE CTA statement at the end of the email to address the 'clear_cta' scoring issues in test cases devrel_vp_mid and devrel_manager_enterprise, which both noted 'Too many CTA signals — feels pushy'. This targets the lowest-scoring criterion (5/10) while maintaining all other requirements.

Added explicit instruction to avoid the word 'leverage' and other common buzzwords, targeting the 'no_banned_phrases' criterion which scored 7/10 in test case community_lead_no_hook. Also added guidance to strengthen subject lines by making them more specific and outcome-focused, addressing the 'subject_quality' criterion which scored 5-8/10 across multiple test cases. This dual improvement targets the two most frequently penalized areas without disrupting the successful CTA structure.

Adding explicit guidance to strengthen personalization by connecting the research hook to specific pain points. The 'personalization' criterion scored only 6/10 in two test cases (devrel_head_large and devrel_vp_mid), making it the most frequently low-scoring criterion. The new instruction emphasizes creating a logical bridge from the research hook to the pain point, ensuring the email feels tailored rather than templated.
